issues.name: Sitemap Domain
issues.description: Checks that all sitemap URLs belong to the expected domain
issues.solution: All URLs in your sitemap should point to pages on your own domain. Cross-domain URLs in sitemaps are a configuration error - search engines will ignore URLs that don't match the sitemap's domain. Remove external URLs from your sitemap or fix the domain in URLs if they're incorrectly formatted.

issues.name: Sitemap Coverage
issues.description: Checks for indexable pages that are not in the sitemap
issues.solution: Your sitemap should include all pages you want search engines to index. Pages that are crawlable and indexable (no noindex, not blocked by robots.txt) should generally be in your sitemap. Missing pages may not be discovered or indexed efficiently. Use a sitemap generator that automatically includes all indexable pages, or manually add important pages.

issues.name: HTTPS
issues.description: Checks for HTTPS usage
issues.solution: HTTPS encrypts data between users and your server, protecting sensitive information. It's a ranking signal and required for many modern browser features. Migrate to HTTPS by obtaining an SSL certificate (free from Let's Encrypt). Update internal links to use https://. Set up 301 redirects from HTTP to HTTPS. Update your canonical URLs and sitemap. Check for mixed content warnings after migration.

issues.name: Content Security Policy
issues.description: Checks for Content-Security-Policy header and validates directives
issues.solution: CSP prevents XSS attacks by restricting which resources can load. Start with a report-only policy to identify issues. Key directives: default-src 'self', script-src (avoid 'unsafe-inline'), img-src, style-src, frame-ancestors. Use nonces or hashes instead of 'unsafe-inline' for scripts. Test thoroughly as strict CSP can break functionality.

checks.name: csp-missing
checks.legacyValue: Site vulnerable to XSS without CSP

issues.name: Keyword Stuffing
issues.description: Detects excessive keyword repetition in content
issues.solution: Keyword stuffing is repeating words unnaturally to manipulate rankings. Search engines penalize this practice. Write naturally for users first. Use keywords where they fit naturally. Aim for 1-2% keyword density at most. Use synonyms and related terms instead of repeating the exact same phrase. Focus on providing value, not gaming algorithms.

issues.name: Word Count
issues.description: Checks content length for thin content issues
issues.solution: Pages with thin content (under 300 words) often struggle to rank well and are actively deindexed by Google since the June 2025 core update. Add more valuable, relevant content to thin pages—aim for at least 500 words for standard pages and 1000+ for in-depth articles. If a page can't be fleshed out, voluntarily noindex it or consolidate it into a more comprehensive resource. Trimming thin pages from your index is better than leaving low-value content for Google to penalize.

issues.name: Offscreen Image Lazy Loading
issues.description: Checks if offscreen images use lazy loading
issues.solution: Add loading='lazy' to images below the fold to defer loading until needed. This reduces initial page load time and saves bandwidth. Exception: Don't lazy-load LCP image or above-the-fold content. Use loading='eager' for critical images.

issues.name: Image Dimensions
issues.description: Checks for width/height attributes (prevents CLS)
issues.solution: Specifying width and height attributes prevents Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) by reserving space before images load. Add width and height attributes to img tags matching the image's intrinsic dimensions. Use CSS for responsive sizing if needed. For responsive images, the aspect ratio from width/height prevents layout shifts even when CSS overrides the actual size.

issues.name: JavaScript File Size Too Large
issues.description: Checks for JavaScript files that exceed recommended size limits
issues.solution: Large JavaScript files block the main thread and delay interactivity. Code-split bundles into smaller chunks, tree-shake unused exports, lazy-load non-critical scripts, and defer or async load third-party scripts. Use dynamic imports for route-based splitting.

issues.name: LCP Optimization Hints
issues.description: Checks for Largest Contentful Paint optimization opportunities
issues.solution: LCP measures when the largest content element becomes visible. Optimize by: 1) Preload your LCP image with <link rel='preload' as='image'>. 2) Don't use loading='lazy' on above-fold images as it delays loading. 3) Minimize render-blocking CSS/JS in <head>. 4) Use modern image formats (WebP/AVIF) for faster loading. 5) Consider using fetchpriority='high' on the LCP image.

issues.name: Time to First Byte
issues.description: Measures server response time (TTFB)
issues.solution:
Time to First Byte (TTFB) measures how quickly your server responds. Slow TTFB indicates server/backend issues.

Thresholds (Core Web Vitals):
- Good: < 600ms
- Needs improvement: 600-1000ms
- Poor: > 1000ms

Fixes for slow TTFB:
- Enable server caching (Redis, Varnish, CDN)
- Optimize database queries
- Use CDN for static assets
- Upgrade server resources
- Reduce server-side processing
- Enable HTTP/2 or HTTP/3
- Use edge computing (Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge)

issues.name: Total Page Weight
issues.description: Checks the total byte weight of the page
issues.solution: Reduce total page weight for faster loads on slow connections. Optimize images (use modern formats, compress, serve appropriate sizes). Minify and compress CSS/JS. Remove unused code via tree-shaking. Lazy-load non-critical resources. Target under 1.6MB for mobile users.

issues.name: Critical Request Chains
issues.description: Identifies chains of dependent resources that delay rendering
issues.solution: Critical request chains are sequences of dependent network requests that must complete before the page can render. Reduce chain depth by: 1) Inlining critical CSS instead of linking external files. 2) Adding async or defer to non-critical scripts. 3) Avoiding CSS @import — use <link> tags instead. 4) Using <link rel='preload'> for critical resources. 5) Reducing the number of render-blocking resources in <head>.

issues.name: Duplicate JavaScript
issues.description: Detects duplicate JavaScript libraries loaded multiple times
issues.solution: Remove duplicate JavaScript library loads to reduce page weight and avoid conflicts. Check for the same library loaded from different CDNs or versions. Use a single source for each dependency. Consider using a module bundler to deduplicate shared dependencies.

details.note: Remove duplicates to reduce page weight

issues.name: Lazy Loading Above Fold
issues.description: Detects lazy loading on likely above-fold images
issues.solution: Don't use loading='lazy' on images that appear above the fold (visible without scrolling). Lazy loading these images delays LCP because the browser waits for layout before fetching. For hero images and LCP candidates: 1) Remove loading='lazy'. 2) Add fetchpriority='high'. 3) Consider preloading with <link rel='preload' as='image'>. Only use lazy loading for below-fold images.

issues.name: Unminified JavaScript
issues.description: Detects unminified JavaScript that could be optimized
issues.solution: Minify JavaScript to reduce file size and improve load times. Use build tools like Terser, esbuild, or UglifyJS. Most bundlers (Webpack, Vite, Rollup) minify automatically in production. Minification shortens variable names, removes whitespace, and dead code.

meta.reason: high newlines (1.58%), 81 comments, long function names, formatted code, excessive whitespace
high newlines (1.65%), 17 comments, long function names, formatted code, excessive whitespace
high newlines (2.10%), 192 comments, long function names, formatted code, excessive whitespace
high newlines (2.05%), 3213 comments, long variable names, long function names, formatted code, excessive whitespace

issues.name: Cache Headers
issues.description: Checks Cache-Control header configuration
issues.solution: Use Cache-Control headers to enable browser caching. For static assets (CSS, JS, images), set long max-age (1 year) with immutable when using hashed filenames. For HTML, use shorter max-age or no-cache with revalidation. Example: 'Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable' for versioned assets.

issues.name: HTTP/2
issues.description: Checks for HTTP/2 protocol support
issues.solution: HTTP/2 enables multiplexing, header compression, and server push for faster page loads. Most modern web servers and CDNs support HTTP/2 out of the box. Requires HTTPS. Check your server/CDN documentation to enable it. HTTP/3 (QUIC) provides even better performance.

issues.name: Source Maps
issues.description: Checks for source map availability and configuration
issues.solution: Source maps help debug minified code but can expose source code if publicly accessible. For production: 1) Either remove source maps entirely, 2) Restrict access via server config, or 3) Use 'hidden' source maps uploaded only to error tracking services. Exposed source maps can reveal business logic and security implementations to attackers.

details.note: Source maps may expose original source code. Verify these URLs are not publicly accessible.
Inline source maps increase bundle size and expose code

issues.name: OG Image Size
issues.description: Checks og:image meets recommended size (1200x630)
issues.solution: og:image should be at least 1200x630 pixels for optimal display on Facebook and LinkedIn. Smaller images may appear cropped or low quality. Use 1.91:1 aspect ratio. Keep file size under 8MB. Test with Facebook Sharing Debugger. Consider creating dedicated social images for key pages.

issues.name: Duplicate ID ARIA
issues.description: Checks that IDs used in ARIA attributes are unique
issues.solution: IDs referenced by ARIA attributes (aria-labelledby, aria-describedby, aria-controls, etc.) must be unique on the page. Duplicate IDs cause assistive technology to potentially reference the wrong element. Rename duplicate IDs to be unique.

issues.name: Select Name
issues.description: Checks that select elements have accessible names
issues.solution: Select elements need accessible labels. Use <label for='selectId'>Label</label>, wrap in <label>, or use aria-label/aria-labelledby.

issues.name: ARIA Hidden Focus
issues.description: Ensures aria-hidden elements do not contain focusable content
issues.solution: Elements with aria-hidden='true' should not contain focusable content. When an element is hidden from assistive technology but still focusable, keyboard users can tab to it but screen reader users won't know what they're interacting with. Either remove aria-hidden or make children non-focusable with tabindex='-1'.

issues.name: ARIA Input Field Name
issues.description: Checks that input fields have accessible names
issues.solution: All input fields need accessible names. Best options: 1) Use <label for='inputId'>. 2) Use aria-label or aria-labelledby. 3) Wrap input in <label>. Placeholder alone is not sufficient as it disappears when typing.

issues.name: ARIA Toggle Field Name
issues.description: Checks that toggle fields (checkbox, radio, switch) have accessible names
issues.solution: Toggle fields need accessible names to describe what they control. Use <label for='id'>, aria-label, aria-labelledby, or wrap in <label>. Example: <label><input type='checkbox'> Subscribe to newsletter</label>

issues.name: Label Content Name Mismatch
issues.description: Checks that visible label text is part of accessible name
issues.solution: For controls with visible labels, the accessible name should contain the visible text. Voice control users say what they see - if the accessible name doesn't include the visible label, voice commands won't work. Example: A button showing 'Search' should not have aria-label='Find products'.

checks.name: label-content-name-mismatch
checks.message: 2 element(s) where visible text doesn't match accessible name

items.id: button: visible="tunai / selesai di t" vs aria-label="bayar tunai atau di "
button: visible="qris (bayar sekarang" vs aria-label="bayar via qris"

details.issue: Voice control users may not be able to activate these controls

issues.name: ARIA Labels
issues.description: Checks that interactive elements have accessible names
issues.solution: All interactive elements (buttons, links, inputs) need accessible names for screen readers. Use: visible text content, aria-label for icon-only buttons, aria-labelledby to reference existing text, or the title attribute as a fallback. Icon buttons especially need aria-label: <button aria-label='Close'>×</button>. Test with a screen reader or browser accessibility inspector.

issues.name: Form Labels
issues.description: Checks that form inputs have associated labels
issues.solution: Every form input needs an accessible label for screen readers. Options: 1) Use <label for='inputId'>Label</label> with matching id. 2) Wrap the input inside <label>Label <input></label>. 3) Use aria-label or aria-labelledby for inputs where visible labels aren't feasible. Placeholders are not sufficient substitutes for labels. Hidden inputs, submit buttons, and image buttons don't need labels.

issues.name: Color Contrast
issues.description: Checks for color contrast issues in styles and classes
issues.solution: Text must have sufficient contrast with its background for readability. WCAG AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text (18px+ or 14px+ bold). Use tools like WebAIM Contrast Checker to verify. Common issues: light gray text, text over images without overlay. Don't rely on color alone to convey information - add icons or text labels.

items.id: img with class "object-cover grayscale opacity..." may have low contrast
span with class "font-serif text-8xl md:text-9x..." may have low contrast
input with class "h-9 w-full min-w-0 rounded-4xl..." may have low contrast
div with class "flex shrink-0 items-center jus..." may have low contrast
input with class "h-full w-full min-w-0 bg-trans..." may have low contrast
button with class "group/button inline-flex shrin..." may have low contrast
button with class "flex items-center justify-betw..." may have low contrast
svg with class "pointer-events-none size-4 tex..." may have low contrast
span with class "hidden sm:inline opacity-20 fo..." may have low contrast

details.note: WCAG AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text

issues.name: About Page
issues.description: Checks for an about/company page with content
issues.solution: An About page establishes credibility and trust. Include company history, mission, team overview, and credentials. Link from main navigation or footer. For E-E-A-T, explain your expertise and why visitors should trust you. Include contact information and physical location if applicable.

issues.name: Author Bylines
issues.description: Checks for visible author names on content pages
issues.solution: Author bylines demonstrate experience and accountability. Show author names prominently on articles, blog posts, and expert content. Include author credentials where relevant. Link author names to bio pages. For YMYL content (health, finance), author transparency is especially important for Google's E-E-A-T assessment.

checks.name: author-byline
checks.message: No content pages have author attribution
checks.legacyValue: Add author bylines to build E-E-A-T

issues.name: Contact Page
issues.description: Checks for contact page with multiple contact methods
issues.solution: A contact page with multiple contact methods builds trust. Include: email address or contact form, phone number (if applicable), physical address, and social media links. Make contact information easy to find from any page. For local businesses, include business hours. Response time expectations are also helpful.

issues.name: Content Dates
issues.description: Checks for published and modified dates on content
issues.solution: Visible dates show content freshness and help users assess relevance. Include datePublished and dateModified in Article schema. Show human-readable dates on pages. Update dateModified when making significant changes. Fresh content signals ongoing maintenance and expertise. Stale dates may hurt rankings for time-sensitive topics.
